name: Vermont 3SquaresVT (SNAP)
description: Vermont's food assistance program (SNAP/food stamps) called 3SquaresVT, providing monthly EBT benefits to low-income individuals and families to purchase groceries. Vermont has some of the broadest eligibility in the nation.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting Vermont's SNAP income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 185% FPL for most households

name: Vermont Medicaid (Green Mountain Care)
description: Vermont's Medicaid program providing comprehensive health coverage including doctor visits, hospital care, dental, vision, prescriptions, mental health, and substance use treatment.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income adults, children, pregnant women, seniors, and people with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Adults 19-64: up to 138% FPL. Children: up to 317% FPL. Pregnant women: up to 213% FPL.

name: Vermont Health Connect
description: Vermont's state-based health insurance marketplace where residents can shop for health plans, check Medicaid eligibility, and access premium subsidies for affordable coverage.
eligibilitySummary: Vermont residents seeking health insurance coverage
incomeLimitNotes: Subsidies available for households earning up to 400% FPL
languages: English, and other languages via interpreter services

name: Vermont Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary financial assistance to Vermont workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own. Administered by the Vermont Department of Labor.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost their job through no fault of their own and meet wage and work requirements

name: Vermont Housing Finance Agency (VHFA)
description: Vermont's housing finance agency providing affordable housing programs including rental assistance, homebuyer mortgages, down payment assistance, and housing development financing.
eligibilitySummary: Low-to-moderate income Vermont residents seeking housing assistance
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program and county; generally at or below 80% area median income

name: Vermont Legal Aid
description: Provides free civil legal services to low-income Vermonters in areas including housing, family law, public benefits, disability rights, consumer protection, and elder law.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Vermont residents with civil legal issues
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 200% of federal poverty level

name: Vermont Department of Labor - Career Services
description: Provides workforce development services through local career resource centers including job search assistance, career counseling, skills training, and apprenticeship programs.
eligibilitySummary: All Vermont residents seeking employment or career development services

name: Vermont LIHEAP (Home Heating Fuel Assistance)
description: Helps low-income Vermont households pay for home heating during winter. Vermont's program is especially important given the state's long, cold winters and high heating costs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Vermont households with heating costs
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 185% of federal poverty level
hours: Seasonal program, typically November through April

name: Vermont 211
description: Free, confidential referral service connecting Vermonters to local health and human services including food, housing, utilities, healthcare, and crisis intervention.
eligibilitySummary: All Vermont residents seeking community resource information
languages: English, and other languages via interpreter services

name: Vermont Foodbank
description: Vermont's Feeding America affiliate distributing food to over 300 partner agencies including food pantries, meal sites, shelters, and after-school programs throughout all 14 Vermont counties.
eligibilitySummary: Any Vermont resident experiencing food insecurity

name: Vermont Office of Veterans Affairs
description: Provides services to Vermont veterans including benefits claims assistance, employment support, education benefits, and connections to VA healthcare and state veterans programs.
eligibilitySummary: Vermont veterans and their eligible dependents

name: Vermont Crisis Line (988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline)
description: Vermont's crisis support system providing immediate help for mental health emergencies, suicidal thoughts, and emotional distress. Part of the national 988 Lifeline network.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Vermont experiencing a mental health crisis or emotional distress

name: Vermont Child Care Financial Assistance Program (CCFAP)
description: Provides financial assistance to help Vermont families pay for childcare. Vermont has one of the most generous childcare assistance programs in the country.
eligibilitySummary: Vermont families with children in childcare who meet income requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Available to families up to 350% of federal poverty level with sliding scale co-pays

name: Vermont Reach Up (TANF)
description: Vermont's TANF cash assistance program for low-income families with children, providing monthly benefits, case management, employment services, and educational opportunities.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with dependent children meeting participation requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by family size

name: Vermont WIC
description: Provides nutrition education, breastfeeding support, healthy food benefits, and healthcare referrals for low-income pregnant and postpartum women, infants, and children up to age 5.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, breastfeeding, or postpartum women, infants, and children under 5 at nutritional risk
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 185% of federal poverty level

name: Vermont Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides free home energy efficiency improvements to low-income Vermont households including insulation, air sealing, heating system repairs, and energy audits to reduce utility costs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters, with priority for elderly, disabled, and families with children
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 200% of federal poverty level

name: Vermont Division of Vocational Rehabilitation
description: Provides employment services to Vermonters with disabilities including career counseling, skills training, assistive technology, and job placement to find and maintain employment.
eligibilitySummary: Vermont residents with disabilities that create barriers to employment

name: Green Mountain Transit
description: Public transit service for the Burlington metro area and central Vermont providing fixed-route bus service, commuter routes, and paratransit. Free fare on many routes.
eligibilitySummary: All Vermont residents; many routes are fare-free

name: Vermont Department of Aging and Independent Living
description: Provides services for Vermont seniors including Area Agency on Aging programs, Meals on Wheels, senior centers, caregiver support, and long-term care options counseling.
eligibilitySummary: Vermont residents age 60 and older, and their caregivers

name: Vermont Network Against Domestic and Sexual Violence
description: Statewide coalition coordinating domestic violence and sexual assault services, providing a crisis hotline, and connecting survivors to local shelters and advocacy programs across Vermont.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing domestic violence, sexual assault, or stalking in Vermont

name: Disability Rights Vermont
description: Vermont's designated protection and advocacy agency providing free legal services to people with disabilities on issues including access to services, abuse and neglect, and civil rights.
eligibilitySummary: Vermont residents with disabilities facing rights violations or needing legal advocacy

name: Vermont General Assistance
description: Emergency cash assistance for Vermonters in crisis, including emergency housing, disaster relief, and basic needs support for those not eligible for other programs.
eligibilitySummary: Vermont residents in emergency situations who have exhausted other resources

name: Vermont Head Start
description: Comprehensive early childhood education for low-income Vermont families providing education, health screenings, nutrition, and family support for children birth to age 5.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with children birth to age 5 and pregnant women
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 100% of federal poverty level; categorical eligibility for certain groups

name: Vermont Adult Learning
description: Provides free adult education including GED preparation, English language learning, digital literacy, financial literacy, and workforce readiness programs throughout Vermont.
eligibilitySummary: Vermont adults age 16 and older not enrolled in school

name: COTS (Committee on Temporary Shelter) - Burlington
description: Provides emergency shelter, transitional housing, permanent supportive housing, and homelessness prevention services for individuals and families in the greater Burlington area.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families experiencing homelessness in the greater Burlington area

name: Chittenden Emergency Food Shelf (Burlington)
description: Provides free groceries to individuals and families in Chittenden County through food distribution, community meals, and nutrition education programs.
eligibilitySummary: Chittenden County residents experiencing food insecurity

name: Champlain Valley Office of Economic Opportunity (CVOEO)
description: Community action agency serving northwest Vermont providing energy assistance, food programs, housing counseling, financial literacy, micro-business development, and tax preparation.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of Chittenden, Addison, Franklin, and Grand Isle counties

name: Vermont Everyone Eats
description: Innovative Vermont program connecting local restaurants with community members facing food insecurity, providing free nutritious meals prepared by local restaurants and supporting the local food economy.
eligibilitySummary: Any Vermont resident experiencing food insecurity; no documentation required

name: Vermont Elective Home Energy Assistance Team (HEAT)
description: Provides emergency fuel delivery and crisis heating assistance when Vermont households face heating emergencies such as running out of fuel or facing utility disconnection.
eligibilitySummary: Vermont households facing heating emergencies regardless of income
incomeLimitNotes: Income guidelines apply but emergency assistance may be available above normal limits

name: Vermont Rental Assistance Program
description: Provides emergency rental assistance to Vermont tenants at risk of eviction, covering past-due rent and utility payments to maintain housing stability.
eligibilitySummary: Vermont renters at risk of eviction with household income at or below 80% AMI
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 80% area median income
